# Food Processing Factory Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-food-processing-food-processing-factory
**Vertical slug:** food-processing
**VN-ID:** VN-AGR-PRO-002
**Niche family:** NF-AGR-PRO (standalone agricultural processing)
**Family role:** standalone
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's food processing sector employs over 5 million people and encompasses grain milling (maize, sorghum, wheat), cassava processing (garri, fufu, starch), palm kernel cracking, groundnut oil extraction, tomato paste, fruit juice production, and packaged snacks. NAFDAC registration is mandatory for all packaged food products. FMARD (Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development) regulates primary processing. SON certifies manufacturing standards. Target buyers include supermarket chains (Shoprite, Spar, Justrite), hotel chains, catering companies, and export buyers. WhatsApp B2B enquiry is the primary contact for bulk orders.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Supermarket buyer Adaeze, 38, Lagos**: Sources packaged garri, groundnut oil, and palm oil for chain stores. Needs NAFDAC number, SON cert, and halal certification (for certain products). WhatsApps factory for price list.
- **Export trader Mr Ibrahim, 52, Kano**: Looking to source processed groundnut oil and sesame paste for West African export. Needs NAFDAC, FMARD export permit, and quality assurance docs.
- **Hotel procurement officer Funke, 35, Abuja**: Needs monthly supply of bottled water, fruit juice, and packaged condiments. Requires consistent quality and delivery.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home**: Hero (factory name, "NAFDAC-Registered Food Processor"), WhatsApp bulk order CTA, featured products (packaged items with ₦ bulk pricing), trust strip (NAFDAC, SON, FMARD, halal cert), export-ready badge
- **About**: Factory capacity, NAFDAC registration, processing standards, export history, location (industrial estate/LGA)
- **Products**: Full product catalogue with NAFDAC numbers, pack sizes, ₦ pricing (wholesale/retail), MOQ
- **Contact**: WhatsApp bulk enquiry (primary), factory address, purchase order form (NDPR-compliant)

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Professional, compliance-forward, and export-ready. "NAFDAC Certified. Quality You Can Trust", "Farm-to-Factory Processing", "Export-Ready Products". Primary CTA: "Request a Price List via WhatsApp". Secondary: "View Product Catalogue". Avoid: generic western food imagery.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- NAFDAC product registration numbers (displayed per product)
- SON manufacturing standards certificate
- FMARD processing licence
- Halal certification (where applicable)
- Export permit / NEPC registration
- ISO food safety certification (if applicable)
- Supermarket supply references

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Factory floor — processing line, workers in hairnets, Nigerian industrial setting
2. NAFDAC-certified product display — packaged garri, palm oil, groundnut oil with NAFDAC numbers visible
3. Quality control inspection — lab-coat worker checking product, Nigerian food factory
4. Loading dock — products being loaded into delivery trucks, branded packaging
5. Raw material intake — farmers delivering fresh cassava or groundnuts to factory gate
6. Export pallet — branded products stacked for export, NEPC sticker visible
7. NAFDAC certificate — framed on factory wall, primary trust signal

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- [ ] NAFDAC product registration number per product
- [ ] SON manufacturing standards certificate
- [ ] FMARD processing licence
- [ ] Halal certification (MAN/NAFDAC-recognised body)
- [ ] NEPC export registration
- [ ] NDPR consent on purchase order form
- [ ] Factory health and safety compliance

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp bulk order CTA prominent
- [x] NAFDAC numbers displayed per product
- [x] Nigerian food products (garri, palm oil, groundnut oil, cassava)
- [x] ₦ wholesale pricing with MOQ
- [x] Export-ready and supermarket supply contexts
- [x] Mobile-first layout
- [x] esc() on all inputs
- [x] NDPR on purchase order form
